hypothesis: Mutation of position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 interactions while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 binding. Mutation of position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will introduce electrostatic complementarity with SSTR2 ECL3 (284/286) while disrupting off-target electrostatic interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding and selectivity but are failing due to poor structural quality. Tightening structural filters and introducing mutations at non-pharmacophore positions will improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current clash threshold is too lenient, allowing candidates with poor structural quality (e.g., iter19_cand007 has pLDDT=0.0). Tightening the clash score will enforce better geometry and reduce structural failures.

To improve selectivity and push Δmargin further positive, we should focus on mutating non-pharmacophore positions that are likely to form off-target contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5. This will help disrupt conserved pocket interactions while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
